name: Jack-o-Lantern Cake
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------DECORATE----------------------------------
2 cups (16 ounces each) Vanilla frosting
2 tablespoons Unsweetened cocoa powder
1 teaspoon Milk
1 1/2 teaspoons Orange paste food color
4 ounces Milk chocolate
1 Brown M&M candy
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour one 10 3/4 cup clean soup can (remove label), and a 6 cup bundt pan. If you have two 6 cup bundt pans, bake both halves at the same time. In a large bowl, combine all cake ingredients except flour. Beat with an electric mixer or wooden spoon until blended. Gradually add flour and beat until thick and smooth. Pour 3/4 cup batter into soup can. Pour half of remaining batter (about 3 1/4 cups) into prepared bundt pan. Bake soup can for 30 minutes, bundt pan for 45 minutes or until a pick inserted in the middle comes out clean. Cool in pans on a rack; soup can for 5 minutes, cake for 15 minutes. Invert cakes onto a rack and cool completely. Clean, grease, and flour the bundt pan. Add remaining batter. Bake and cool as above.
To Assemble:
Trim bottoms of bundt cakes with a serrated knife until flat. Place one cake flat side up on a serving plate. Tuck strips of waxed paper under edges to keep the plate clean while frosting. Place other cake flat side down on top, lining up ridges. For Frosting: Mix 1/3 cup vanilla frosting, cocoa powder, and milk in a cup. Scrape rest into a large bowl. Stir in food color (it will darken as it stands). Stem: Cut a 1 1/2 inch thick slice from one end of the soup can cake. Gently push it into the hole in the pumpkin. Top with a dollop of cocoa frosting. Using a small serrated knife, carve the rest of the can cake into a pumpkin stem and press gently onto a frosting dab. Frost stem with remaining cocoa frosting. For a fresh-cut look, cut a thin diagonal slice at the top to reveal the cake inside. Spread orange frosting on the pumpkin from top to bottom using vertical strokes. Cut two large triangles from chocolate bar for eyes and 7 small triangles for teeth. Press into frosting. Use M&M for nose. For leaves, tendrils, etc., melt 6 ounces white baking bars (white chocolate) in a small bowl over simmering water or microwave for 2 minutes. Stir in 1/8 teaspoon green food color paste until blended, then add 3 tablespoons lite corn syrup (mixture will thicken and look grainy). Let it cool slightly before kneading into a pliable consistency. Roll out between sheets of plastic wrap to 1/4 inch thickness. Cut out pumpkin leaves and mark veins with a blunt knife blade. For tendrils, roll small pieces between hands until spaghetti-like strands form, then arrange around a pencil and slip off. Arrange leaves and tendrils on the pumpkin. Use colored frosting to "paint" clothes on Teddy Graham bears for decoration. Only paint one color at a time, letting each dry.
Note 1: Paste food color can be found in cake decorating and party-supply shops and some cookware stores. Add only a tiny amount at a time.
Note 2: If using bears, reserve an additional 1/3 cup vanilla frosting for their clothes.
